name: 杭州
description: Hangzhou, the capital of Zhejiang Province, is famous for its natural beauty and historical culture. Home to West Lake, a UNESCO World Heritage site, and the ancient water town ambiance of the Grand Canal. Known as 'Paradise on Earth' for its mild climate, lush greenery, and elegant gardens. A leading tech hub as headquarters of Alibaba.

climate.tips: Best visited in spring for flowers or autumn for golden foliage. Summer is hot and humid with frequent rain. Spring rainy season (Meiyu) in June. Wear comfortable shoes for walking around West Lake.

attractions.name: 西湖
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site and the iconic heart of Hangzhou. A freshwater lake with surrounding gardens, pagodas, and causeways creating stunning landscapes.
attractions.address: West Lake Scenic Area, Xihu District
attractions.tips: Rent a bike to circle the lake. Take a boat ride to see the Three Pools. Visit Leifeng Pagoda at sunset. Spring for peach blossoms, autumn for lotus.

attractions.name: 西溪国家湿地公园
attractions.description: The largest urban wetland in China with unique ecosystem, traditional fishing villages, and serene natural beauty.
attractions.address: Xixi National Wetland Park, Xihu District
attractions.tips: Take the boat tour for best experience. Visit in early morning for fewer crowds. Combine with nearby West Lake.

attractions.name: 灵隐寺
attractions.description: One of the oldest and most famous Buddhist temples in China, dating back to 326 AD, with stunning architecture and rock carvings.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid tour groups. The temple is at the foot of Feilai Feng. Wear modest clothing covering shoulders and knees.

attractions.name: 河坊街
attractions.description: A historic pedestrian street in Hangzhou with traditional Chinese architecture, local snacks, tea houses, and souvenir shops.
attractions.tips: Best visited in the evening when illuminated. Try Hangzhou local snacks like lotus root powder and steamed dumplings. Compare prices before buying souvenirs.

attractions.name: 京杭大运河
attractions.description: The longest ancient canal in the world, stretching 1794km from Beijing to Hangzhou. Hangzhou's section offers boat tours through historic water towns.
attractions.tips: Take an evening boat tour for romantic scenery. The bridge views are particularly beautiful at night.

attractions.name: 宋城
attractions.description: A theme park recreating Song Dynasty culture with performances, traditional architecture, and interactive experiences.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance. The 'Legend of the Flying Nymphs' is spectacular. Arrive 30 minutes early for good seats.

attractions.name: 龙井村
attractions.description: Famous for Longjing (Dragon Well) tea, one of China's most prized green teas. Scenic tea plantations and traditional tea houses.
attractions.tips: Visit in early April for tea picking season. Buy tea directly from farmers for authenticity. The village is very peaceful in early morning.

attractions.name: 钱塘江
attractions.description: Famous for the world's largest tidal bore, with monthly tidal waves reaching up to 9 meters during the Mid-Autumn Festival.
attractions.tips: Best viewed during Mid-Autumn Festival (September-October) for the famous tidal bore. The bridge lighting at night is spectacular.

attractions.name: 断桥残雪
attractions.description: One of the Ten Views of West Lake. The bridge appears broken from one angle but whole from another. Snow-covered scenery is especially beautiful.
attractions.address: North of Baidi Bridge, Xihu District
attractions.tips: Best visited in winter after snowfall. The name comes from the optical illusion of a broken arch. Evening lighting creates romantic atmosphere.

attractions.name: 苏堤春晓
attractions.description: One of the Ten Views of West Lake. A 2.7km causeway built by poet Su Dongpo in the 11th century, lined with peach and willow trees.
attractions.tips: Walk or bike the causeway in spring. Each bridge has unique name and view. Start at Hua Gang and end at Nan Peak. Best in early morning.

attractions.name: 曲院风荷
attractions.nameEn: Lotus in the Breeze at Quyuan Garden
attractions.description: One of the Ten Views of West Lake. A classic garden famous for lotus flowers and lotus root viewing in summer.
attractions.address: Northwest of West Lake, Xihu District
attractions.tips: Peak lotus season is July-August. Come early morning for fewer crowds and better photos. The bamboo grove is refreshing in summer.

attractions.name: 平湖秋月
attractions.nameEn: Autumn Moon over the Calm Lake
attractions.description: One of the Ten Views of West Lake. A garden pavilion offering the best view of the full moon reflecting on West Lake.
attractions.tips: Best visited during Mid-Autumn Festival. The garden transforms with lanterns at night. Classic Chinese garden architecture.

attractions.name: 花港观鱼
attractions.description: One of the Ten Views of West Lake. A garden famous for colorful carp in a pond fed by water from Nanhu Peak.
attractions.address: Southwest of West Lake, Xihu District
attractions.tips: The carp are especially active around 10am when fed. Peak peony season is April. The park connects to Su Causeway.

attractions.name: 柳浪闻莺
attractions.description: One of the Ten Views of West Lake. A garden with thousands of willow trees and oriole birds singing in spring.
attractions.address: South shore of West Lake, Xihu District
attractions.tips: Best in early spring when willows turn green. The orioles are most vocal in April-May. Great for morning walks.

attractions.name: 三潭印月
attractions.description: One of the Ten Views of West Lake. A small island with three stone pagodas, iconic symbol of West Lake appearing on the 1 yuan note.
attractions.tips: Take a traditional wooden boat for best experience. At night during Mid-Autumn, 33 moons reflect (one real + 32 from candle light). The island has a excellent tea house.

attractions.name: 雷峰塔
attractions.description: A five-story octagonal pagoda on Sunset Hill, rebuilt in 2002 after the original collapsed in 1924. Famous from Legend of White Snake.
highlights: View of West Lake from top
attractions.tips: Take the escalator to top for panoramic views. Visit at sunset for golden hour photos. The reconstructed pagoda houses archaeological finds.

attractions.name: 千岛湖
attractions.description: A massive reservoir with over 1,000 islands, known for crystal clear water, seafood, and vacation resorts. About 2 hours from Hangzhou.
attractions.tips: Stay overnight at lakeside resort for better experience. Best fresh fish in autumn. The water is clearest in autumn. Ferry between islands is scenic.

attractions.name: 九溪十八涧
attractions.description: A scenic hiking area through bamboo forests, tea plantations, and mountain streams connecting Longjing tea area to West Lake.
attractions.tips: Wear water shoes for stream crossing (5-10 times). Summer is cool retreat from city heat. Best in spring for azaleas. The full hike takes 3-4 hours.

attractions.name: 运河广场
attractions.description: A riverside plaza at the southern end of the Grand Canal in Hangzhou, with historical displays and shopping areas.
attractions.tips: Evening is best when fountain show and lighting are on. The Canal Museum is excellent and free. Connect with boat tour of canal.

attractions.name: 南宋御街
attractions.description: The main street of ancient capital during Southern Song Dynasty, now a pedestrian walkway with historical buildings and shops.
attractions.tips: Start from Nanpeng (Southern Bridge) and walk north. Try local snacks along the way. The street connects to Hefang Street area.

attractions.name: 清河坊
attractions.description: One of the best preserved historical streets in Hangzhou with traditional Hangzhou architecture, shops and traditional crafts.
attractions.tips: Visit in evening for atmosphere. The Wang Yongquan (herbal medicine) shop is famous. Don't miss the traditional Chinese pastry shops.

attractions.name: 良渚古城遗址
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site dating back 5,000 years, representing one of China's earliest civilizations with advanced jade culture.
attractions.tips: Start at the museum for background. The site is outdoors, bring sun protection. Book tickets online. Allow half day to fully explore.

attractions.name: 超山
attractions.description: A scenic mountain famous for plum blossoms in winter and spring, with ancient temples and unique rock formations.
attractions.tips: Peak plum blossom season is February-March. The ancient plum trees near the temple are most impressive. Great for photography.

attractions.name: 钱江新城
attractions.description: Modern business district with iconic architecture including Hangzhou's transformation into a global city. The Hangzhou Olympic Sports Center and Qianjiang Century City feature impressive modern buildings.
attractions.tips: Best visited at night for the spectacular lighting show. The sunset views over Qiantang River are impressive. Take metro Line 4.

attractions.name: 学宫路历史街区
attractions.description: Historic area featuring traditional Chinese architecture, old style streets, and cultural landmarks of the Qing Dynasty.
attractions.tips: Walk in the morning for authentic atmosphere. Try local breakfast spots. Combine with visit to nearby areas.

attractions.name: 香山公园
attractions.description: Beautiful hillside park known for its ancient plum trees, camellia gardens, and panoramic views of West Lake area.
attractions.tips: Best in late winter for plum blossoms (January-February). Cooler temperature due to elevation. Good hiking trails.

attractions.name: 凤凰景宫
attractions.description: Historic palace complex featuring traditional Chinese gardens, imperial architecture, and cultural exhibitions.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby Leifeng Pagoda. Great photography location. Less crowded than main attractions.

attractions.name: 玉皇山
attractions.description: Hilltop temple offering panoramic views of West Lake and the surrounding area. Features Taoist temple and scenic trails.
attractions.tips: Best during sunrise. Combine with Longjing tea village walk. Moderate hiking difficulty. Clear days offer best views.

attractions.name: 杭州剧院
attractions.description: Modern performing arts venue hosting world-class opera, ballet, and theatrical performances. Architectural landmark in Qianjiang.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance. Dress code for performances. Take metro Line 4. Good to combine with Qianjiang night view.

attractions.name: 雷峰夕照
attractions.description: One of the Ten Views of West Lake featuring the sunset view from Leifeng Pagoda. The golden pagoda against sunset is iconic.
attractions.tips: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset for best photos. The golden light on the pagoda is magical. Combine with evening walk around the lake.

restaurants.name: 金沙厅
restaurants.address: 4/F, Four Seasons Hotel Hangzhou, 5 Lingjia Lane, Hubin Road
restaurants.description: One Michelin-starred Zhejiang cuisine with stunning West Lake views. Exceptional seafood and traditional Hangzhou dishes.
dishHighlights: West Lake gold fish in soup

restaurants.name: 外婆家
restaurants.description: Popular chain serving authentic Zhejiang home-style dishes at affordable prices. Great for trying local favorites.

restaurants.name: 知味观
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant established in 1913, famous for Hangzhou cuisine and traditional dim sum.

restaurants.name: 绿茶餐厅
restaurants.description: Trendy restaurant chain known for fresh ingredients and creative Zhejiang dishes in modern atmosphere.

restaurants.name: 奎元馆
restaurants.description: Legendary noodle house established in 1867, serving authentic Hangzhou style noodles with rich broth.

restaurants.name: 楼外楼
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant on the shore of West Lake, established 1848. Famous for view dining and traditional dishes.
dishHighlights: West Lake fish in vinegar sauce

restaurants.name: 龙井菜馆
restaurants.description: Traditional restaurant in tea country specializing in Longjing tea-infused dishes and organic local ingredients.

restaurants.name: 弄堂里
restaurants.description: Popular local chain known for authentic Zhejiang home cooking and comfortable dining environment.

restaurants.name: 龙井茶餐厅
restaurants.description: Scenic restaurant surrounded by tea gardens serving authentic Longjing tea cuisine and local Hangzhou dishes.

restaurants.name: 杭帮菜馆
restaurants.description: Traditional restaurant specializing in authentic Hangzhou cuisine with focus on classic recipes passed down generations.

restaurants.name: 御街宴
restaurants.description: Upscale restaurant near Southern Song Imperial Street serving refined Zhejiang dishes in elegant traditional setting.
dishHighlights: Braised pork belly with tea leaf

restaurants.name: 湖畔大学堂
restaurants.description: Elegant lakeside restaurant with beautiful West Lake views, known for traditional Hangzhou dishes and imperial style cooking.

restaurants.name: 香格里拉中餐厅
restaurants.description: High-end restaurant in Shangri-La hotel serving premium Cantonese and Zhejiang cuisine with professional service.

restaurants.name: 老头儿油爆虾
restaurants.nameEn: Lao Tou Er Oil Explosion Shrimp
restaurants.description: Popular local chain famous for oil explosion shrimp and authentic Hangzhou home-style dishes at reasonable prices.

restaurants.name: 粤浙会
restaurants.description: Upscale restaurant blending Cantonese and Zhejiang cuisines, known for fresh seafood and refined cooking techniques.

restaurants.name: 太子楼
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant near Lingyin Temple serving Buddhist vegetarian cuisine and traditional Hangzhou dishes.

restaurants.name: 运河私房菜
restaurants.description: Hidden gem along the Grand Canal serving secret family recipes of traditional Zhejiang home cooking.

restaurants.name: 南舍私房菜
restaurants.description: Exclusive private kitchen with only 8 tables, serving innovative Zhejiang cuisine with seasonal ingredients.

restaurants.name: 西子湖四季中餐厅
restaurants.address: Four Seasons Resort Hangzhou, 5 Xinghua Road
restaurants.description: Michelin-level dining at Four Seasons Resort with garden and lake views, featuring the finest Zhejiang cuisine.

restaurants.name: 杭州酒家
restaurants.description: Classic Hangzhou restaurant since 1920s, famous for traditional recipes and authentic local flavors.

arrival.from: Major international airports (Shanghai, Beijing, Hong Kong)
arrival.duration: Varies by origin (2-3 hours from Shanghai, 12+ from Europe)
arrival.frequency: Daily international flights from Seoul, Tokyo, Singapore, Bangkok
arrival.tips: Hangzhou Airport is 27km from city center. Airport shuttle bus to East Railway Station and West Lake area. High-speed rail from Shanghai is faster.

arrival.to: Hangzhou East Railway Station / Hangzhou Railway Station
arrival.tips: High-speed train from Shanghai is fastest and most convenient. Hangzhou East is near shopping districts. Hangzhou Station is closer to West Lake.

metro: 12 lines covering city center and suburbs
West Lake area served by Lines 1, 2, 10
bus: Extensive network covering areas not served by metro
Buses to West Lake from main tourist areas
taxi: Didi app recommended for English speakers
bike: Designated bike lanes on West Lake surrounding roads
Scenic bike paths around West Lake

hotels.name: 杭州四季酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book lakeside rooms for West Lake views. Restaurant reservations essential.

hotels.name: 杭州西子湖四季酒店
hotels.nameEn: Four Seasons Resort Hangzhou at West Lake
hotels.bookingTips: Exclusive resort experience. Book months ahead for holidays.

hotels.name: 杭州香格里拉酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great location near West Lake. Weekend packages available.

hotels.name: 杭州君悦酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for business travelers. Check weekend rates.

hotels.name: 杭州西湖饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Book well in advance during peak season.

hotels.name: 杭州希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good base for exploring city. Book river view rooms.

hotels.name: 杭州凯悦酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for first-time visitors. Easy metro access.

hotels.name: 杭州黄龙饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Book standard rooms for best value. Walking distance to West Lake.

hotels.name: 杭州西湖国宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: National leader accommodation. Book months ahead.

hotels.name: 杭州绿城尊蓝钱江豪华精选酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best river view rooms. Walking distance to Broken Bridge.

hotels.name: 杭州JW万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for business travelers. Club lounge access recommended.

hotels.name: 杭州万怡酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great value for money. Metro nearby.

hotels.name: 杭州纳德大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best budget option in city center. Book direct for discounts.

hotels.name: 杭州千岛湖希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book lake view rooms. Great for weekend getaway.

hotels.name: 杭州西溪宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Best combined with wetland visit. Book quiet rooms.

howToUse: Link international credit card or open Chinese account
tips: Works at all restaurants and shops

howToUse: Link international card through WeChat Pay

payment.description: Still useful at traditional markets and small vendors

culturalTips.title: Tea Culture
culturalTips.content: Hangzhou is famous for Longjing (Dragon Well) tea. Visit a tea house for authentic tea ceremony. Spring tea picking (April) is a special experience.

culturalTips.title: Dining Etiquette
culturalTips.content: West Lake vinegar fish and Dongpo pork are local specialties. Tipping is not expected. Restaurant service may be casual.

culturalTips.title: Clothing
culturalTips.content: Comfortable walking shoes essential for West Lake. Dress modestly for temple visits. Light layers for variable weather.

culturalTips.title: Best Times
culturalTips.content: Visit West Lake early morning or late evening to avoid crowds. Weekdays are less crowded than weekends. Spring (April-May) for flowers, autumn for foliage.

culturalTips.title: Silk Heritage
culturalTips.content: Hangzhou has been China's silk capital for over 2,000 years. Visit the China Silk Museum or silk factories for authentic products. '杭州丝绸' is a premium brand recognized worldwide.

culturalTips.title: Lotus and Water Lily Culture
culturalTips.content: Lotus flowers are symbolic in Hangzhou's culture, representing purity and elegance. West Lake's lotus gardens bloom in summer (June-August). The '曲院风荷' view is one of the Ten Views of West Lake.

culturalTips.title: Imperial Heritage
culturalTips.content: Hangzhou served as capital during Southern Song Dynasty (1127-1279). The city's layout, gardens, and cuisine reflect this imperial period. Emperor Gaozong chose Hangzhou for its 'paradise' scenery.

culturalTips.title: Literary and Poetry Heritage
culturalTips.content: West Lake inspired countless poets including Su Dongpo, Bai Juyi, and Li Bai. Many famous poems describe West Lake scenes. The '三潭印月' and '断桥残雪' are classic poetic themes.

culturalTips.title: Longjing Tea Harvest Season
culturalTips.content: The first flush of Longjing tea (明前龙井) in early April is the most prized. Tea picking season brings visitors to Longjing Village. Fresh tea from this period commands premium prices.

culturalTips.title: Legend of White Snake
culturalTips.content: The Legend of White Snake (白蛇传) is Hangzhou's most famous folk tale. Leifeng Pagoda is where the story's heroine was imprisoned. This romantic legend has been adapted into opera, film, and television.

culturalTips.title: Water Town Atmosphere
culturalTips.content: Hangzhou's connection to water includes the Grand Canal, West Lake, and surrounding waterways. Traditional water town architecture can still be seen along canal banks. Boat tours offer glimpses of this heritage.

culturalTips.title: Festival Celebrations
culturalTips.content: Hangzhou celebrates festivals with unique traditions. Mid-Autumn Festival at West Lake features moon viewing and boat rides. Qingming Festival brings visitors to tea gardens and historical sites.

culturalTips.title: Classical Chinese Gardens
culturalTips.content: Hangzhou's classical gardens showcase Chinese landscape design principles. Guo Wang's Garden and Lianhua Chi are excellent examples. Gardens feature pavilions, rock arrangements, and water features.

culturalTips.title: Traditional Crafts
culturalTips.content: Hangzhou preserves traditional crafts including silk weaving, fan making, and bamboo carving. Workshops in Hefang Street demonstrate these skills. Authentic products make meaningful souvenirs.

culturalTips.title: Tech Hub Culture
culturalTips.content: Hangzhou is home to Alibaba and China's tech industry. The city blends traditional culture with innovation. '梦想小镇' (Dream Town) showcases Hangzhou's entrepreneurial spirit.
